results.title: Legal nightmare
results.brief_description: Overviews the legal profession as a whole, in terms of the charges for services they provide and the effect of these fees on the clients who need to seek legal advice. The legal profession in Australia, with its distinction between barristers and solicitors, is full of “archaic rules and traditions” which, when combined with the increasing costs of legal fees, raises the question of justice and its real cost.

results.title: Enter Exit Cave
results.brief_description: Shows the Exit Cave in South-West Tasmania and raises issues concerning the preservation of such sites as part of our heritage. Presents spectacular views of the cave system and its fragile environment. No credits available.

results.title: Paper trail: the life & times of a woodchip
results.creator_credit: Trevor Graham, Sharon Connolly, ABC-TV (Australia), Film Victoria and Yarra Bank Films
results.brief_description: This program examines the devastating environmental effects of the Jappanese paper industry from the woodchipping of Australian forests to the problem of toxic waste produced by Japanese paper mills. Discusses the traditional environmental-safe way of paper making, the attitude to recycling programs in Japan, and the Japanese overuse of paper. If paper use trebles in the next decade, as predicted, Australia being a major supplier of woodchips to Japan, is in danger of losing its forests.

results.title: Nangiloc-Colignan Community Tackles Salinity
results.creator_credit: Dept of Ag and Rural Affairs and Sunraysia Horticultural Centre
results.brief_description: The Nangiloc - Colignan communtiy are working together to tackle the problem of increased salinity in the soil in the Sunraysia district. Farming and irrigation go hand in hand, and with community effort, the aim is to make sure that farming practices enrich rather tahn deplete a vital resource.

results.title: Researching the topic
results.brief_description:
Episode number 2 of Series “Finding out”.
Demonstrates the principles of student research through the experiences of a secondary student who is required to do a research project on any aspect of “people and work”. As the practicalities of the task are confronted the audience is guided through the key stages of project planning, information collection and processing, formulating conclusions and presentation. Accompanied by 13p Teacher Notes.

results.title: Preparing and conducting an interview
results.creator_credit: Justin Shortal and Video Education Australasia
results.brief_description:
Episode number 1 of Series “Finding out”.
Kylie’s class has been set the task of recording an interview with an aspect of their own choice. We follow Kylie’s progress as she moves through a process of preparing and conducting an interview.

results.title: Greenhouse effect: we can all make a difference
results.brief_description: An animated collage of fast-moving images which focuses on the problems of the Green-house effect and what solutions can be tried. Would provide an interesting starting point for discussion with upper primary/lower secondary students.

results.title: Soil Conservation District: Jerramungup
results.creator_credit: Gerry North and Western Australia. Dept. of Agriculture
results.brief_description: Describes how each committee member deals with a land degredation problem. Problems discussed and projects undertaken: wind erosion, tree and saltbush planting, direct drilling research, reduction of salinity and waterlogging in catchment area, growing of lucerne to reduce saline watertable.

results.title: Soil structure decline
results.creator_credit: Gerry North and Western Australia. Dept. of Agriculture
results.brief_description: After a short period of cultivationn, our soils are losing their inherent structure and are becoming difficult to work. This program discusses soil structure decline and its effect on agricultural productivity.
